metadata.archetype_justification: Application owns persistent state (MySQL/RDS database with 9 tables), exposes CRUD operations on business entities (orders, inventory, payments, returns, users), and manages entity lifecycle (order status transitions). Classified as stateful-crud.
metadata.context: PHP monolith with Docker and CloudFormation on App Runner — containerize and expose inventory APIs the agent needs for restocking decisions.

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith — 3192 lines in single PHP file, all business domains sharing one database with no module boundaries
top_gaps.impact: Blocks independent scaling, deployment, and team autonomy; triggers Move to Cloud Native pathway

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: No CI/CD pipeline — deployment is a manual bash script
top_gaps.impact: Manual deployments are error-prone, slow, and prevent rapid iteration; triggers Move to Modern DevOps pathway

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: Plaintext fallback credentials in source code
top_gaps.impact: Critical security vulnerability — credentials exposed in version control

top_gaps.title: Integration Testing
top_gaps.gap: No test files found anywhere in the repository
top_gaps.impact: Zero regression safety net; any change risks breaking production

top_gaps.title: Unstructured Data Storage
top_gaps.gap: No managed object storage; product images referenced as filesystem paths
top_gaps.impact: Unstructured data locked in inaccessible storage; no path to AI/analytics integration

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Monolith is functional and deployed; context requires independent inventory API; EKS+Terraform+GitOps preferences align with incremental extraction

findings.title: No workflow orchestration for multi-step order lifecycle
findings.description: Multi-step business operations (order creation, fulfillment lifecycle) are implemented as database transactions and hardcoded state machines in application code. No dedicated workflow orchestration service.
findings.gap: Order fulfillment workflow is a simple state machine in code with no dedicated orchestration service for error recovery or visibility.
findings.recommendation: Introduce AWS Step Functions for the order fulfillment workflow when decomposing the monolith.

findings.title: No async messaging or streaming infrastructure
findings.description: No messaging or streaming infrastructure exists. All operations are synchronous within the monolith. No SQS, SNS, EventBridge, or any message broker.
findings.gap: No messaging where state changes cross domain boundaries. Cross-domain state changes have no async path.
findings.recommendation: Introduce Amazon EventBridge for domain event publishing when decomposing the monolith. Start with inventory.stock-level-changed and order.status-changed events.

findings.title: Network security lacks managed networking services
findings.description: VPC with private subnets, least-privilege security groups, non-default DB port. VPC Flow Logs enabled. Missing VPC endpoints and managed networking services.
findings.gap: No managed networking services (VPC endpoints, PrivateLink, VPC Lattice). Broad HTTPS egress rule on DB security group.
findings.recommendation: Add VPC endpoints for AWS services (ECR, CloudWatch Logs, Secrets Manager). Consider VPC Lattice for service-to-service communication.

findings.title: No API Gateway for managed API entry point
findings.description: WAF Web ACL provides IP whitelisting and exploit protection. App Runner provides HTTPS endpoint. No API Gateway with throttling, request validation, or usage plans.
findings.gap: No dedicated API Gateway with per-route throttling, request validation, or usage plans.
findings.recommendation: Deploy Amazon API Gateway in front of the application for throttling, request validation, and API management.

findings.title: Auto-scaling limited to compute with default settings
findings.description: App Runner auto-scaling configured (1-3 instances, MaxConcurrency: 100). No database scaling. No custom scaling policies.
findings.gap: Auto-scaling uses default settings. No scaling on data layer. Database is fixed db.t3.micro.
findings.recommendation: Consider Aurora MySQL with auto-scaling read replicas. Add custom CloudWatch metrics for more intelligent scaling.

findings.title: Backup configured but no restore testing or cross-region replication
findings.description: RDS automated backups with 7-day retention, PITR enabled, deletion protection. No documented restore testing or cross-region backup.
findings.gap: No documented restore testing procedures. No cross-region backup replication. No AWS Backup plan.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS Backup plan. Document and test restore procedures. Consider cross-region replication.

findings.title: IaC covers primary resources but lacks operational resources
findings.description: CloudFormation covers VPC, RDS, App Runner, WAF, ECR, IAM, KMS. Missing: CloudWatch alarms, Secrets Manager, Backup plans, Route 53.
findings.gap: No monitoring alarms, secrets management, or operational resources in IaC.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Terraform (preferred). Extend coverage to CloudWatch alarms, Secrets Manager, AWS Backup. Adopt GitOps workflow.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline — all deployments manual
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline exists. Deployment is a manual bash script (deploy.sh). AWS deployment follows manual CLI instructions.
findings.gap: All deployments are manual. No automated build, test, or deploy stages. No pipeline for IaC changes.
findings.recommendation: Implement CI/CD with GitHub Actions. Add Terraform CI with plan/apply workflow. Integrate ArgoCD for EKS deployments.

findings.title: PHP has limited cloud-native tooling depth
findings.description: PHP 8.2 on Apache. No Composer dependency management. No modern framework. Functional AWS SDK but narrower cloud-native ecosystem.
findings.gap: PHP has narrower cloud-native tooling. No dependency management. No framework.
findings.recommendation: Consider TypeScript/Node.js or Python for new extracted services. PHP can remain for the monolith during migration.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolith with no module boundaries
findings.description: Single 3192-line PHP file containing all business domains. No module boundaries, no separation of concerns. 9 database tables accessed directly throughout.
findings.gap: No module boundaries. All business logic, data access, routing, authentication, and UI in one file. Pervasive shared state.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition starting with inventory domain. Extract to EKS with own Aurora database.

findings.title: All communication synchronous with no async patterns
findings.description: All domain interactions are synchronous in-process calls within HTTP request cycles. No event publishing, no message queues.
findings.gap: All communication synchronous. Cross-domain state changes have no async path for decoupling upon decomposition.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge for domain event publishing as services are extracted.

findings.title: All operations synchronous regardless of duration
findings.description: All operations execute synchronously within HTTP request cycle. No background job processing, no status polling, no callback patterns.
findings.gap: No patterns for async job processing. Blocking calls create timeout risks as system scales.
findings.recommendation: Implement async patterns for operations that may exceed response time budgets. Use SQS + Lambda or EKS worker pods.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: No versioning. Routes are unversioned (/api/products, /api/orders). No version prefix, headers, or changelog.
findings.gap: No versioning strategy — breaking changes deployed directly. Critical gap for agent API consumption.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-path versioning (/v1/) when extracting inventory service. API Gateway supports stage-based versioning.

findings.title: Environment variables for endpoints with no dynamic discovery
findings.description: Database endpoint configured via environment variables. No dynamic service discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: Environment variables only. No dynamic discovery for when microservices are extracted.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Kubernetes-native service discovery (CoreDNS in EKS) and API Gateway as service catalog.

findings.title: No managed object storage for unstructured data
findings.description: No S3 bucket. Product images referenced as filesystem paths in database. No document parsing pipeline.
findings.gap: Unstructured data locked in inaccessible filesystem paths. No managed storage or parsing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Create S3 bucket for product images. Serve via CloudFront. Store metadata in DynamoDB or Aurora.

findings.title: No data access layer — raw SQL scattered throughout code
findings.description: Raw PDO queries scattered throughout the single PHP file. No repository pattern, no ORM, no data contract.
findings.gap: Database queries throughout code with no abstraction. No separation between business logic and data access.
findings.recommendation: Implement repository/DAO pattern per extracted service. Use appropriate ORM for chosen language.

findings.title: Database version pinned but no upgrade procedure documented
findings.description: MySQL 8.4.8 pinned in CloudFormation. Not approaching EOL. Local dev uses MySQL 8.0 (version mismatch).
findings.gap: No documented version-update procedure. Local/production version mismatch (8.0 vs 8.4.8).
findings.recommendation: Document upgrade procedure. Align local dev to MySQL 8.4. Consider Aurora MySQL.

findings.title: RDS uses AWS-managed encryption key instead of customer-managed
findings.description: RDS encrypted with AWS-managed key. ECR and VPC Flow Logs use customer-managed KMS keys with rotation.
findings.gap: Primary data store (RDS) lacks customer-managed KMS key. No centralized key management documentation.
findings.recommendation: Add customer-managed KMS key for RDS encryption. Document key rotation policy.

findings.title: Session-based auth instead of token-based (OAuth2/JWT)
findings.description: PHP session-based authentication. All API routes require session. No OAuth2/JWT. WAF provides IP whitelisting.
findings.gap: No token-based auth. Session-based auth unsuitable for agent/API consumption.
findings.recommendation: Implement OAuth2/JWT via Amazon Cognito with API Gateway integration.

findings.title: Application manages own authentication with no external IdP
findings.description: Users stored in local database with bcrypt passwords. PHP session login. No external IdP integration.
findings.gap: No external IdP integration. Standalone auth cannot participate in centralized access policies or SSO.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for centralized identity management, OAuth2/OIDC, and federation.

findings.title: Plaintext credentials in source code
findings.description: Hardcoded fallback password in get_db() function. Seed passwords in plaintext. DB credentials passed as plain environment variables.
findings.gap: Plaintext credentials in source. No Secrets Manager. No rotation.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Move to AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation. Update App Runner to pull secrets at runtime.

findings.title: Default base image with no hardening
findings.description: Good container security practices (non-root, read-only, no-new-privileges). ECR scan-on-push. But default php:8.2-apache image, no CI/CD integration.
findings.gap: Default base image not hardened. Vulnerability scanning not in automated workflow.
findings.recommendation: Switch to hardened base image. Gate deployments on ECR scan results once CI/CD established.

findings.title: No application security scanning
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline, no SAST, no DAST, no dependency scanning. ECR scan-on-push is only security scanning (manual trigger).
findings.gap: No security scanning in any automated workflow.
findings.recommendation: Add SAST (Semgrep), container scanning gate, and dependency scanning to CI/CD pipeline.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No X-Ray, no OpenTelemetry, no trace ID propagation. Only basic App Runner request logging.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. Cross-service debugging will be impossible without tracing.
findings.recommendation: Instrument OpenTelemetry in extracted services. Use ADOT on EKS for automatic trace collection.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No SLOs, no CloudWatch alarms, no latency/error thresholds defined. Basic App Runner health checks only.
findings.gap: No formal SLOs. Cannot measure whether system meets user expectations.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for inventory API: p99 latency < 500ms, availability > 99.9%. Create CloudWatch alarms.

findings.title: No business metrics published
findings.description: No custom CloudWatch metrics. No business KPI dashboards. Only default infrastructure metrics.
findings.gap: No business outcome metrics. Cannot measure value delivery.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom metrics: orders-per-hour, inventory-stock-level, payment-success-rate.

findings.title: No alerting configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms. No anomaly detection. No incident notification integration.
findings.gap: Degradation and failures go unnoticed. No alerting of any kind.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for 5xx error rate, p99 latency, RDS CPU, free storage. Add anomaly detection.

findings.title: Rolling deployments with no canary or traffic shifting
findings.description: App Runner rolling deployments with basic health checks. No canary, no blue/green, no automated rollback.
findings.gap: No staged rollout or traffic shifting. No automated rollback on metrics degradation.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments with Argo Rollouts on EKS. Automated rollback on error rate.

findings.title: Zero automated testing
findings.description: No test files anywhere. No unit tests, integration tests, API tests, or contract tests. Zero coverage.
findings.gap: Every deployment carries full regression risk. Cannot verify behavioral parity during decomposition.
findings.recommendation: Add integration tests for critical APIs immediately. Gate deployments on test passage.

findings.title: No incident response procedures or automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation documents, no Lambda remediation, no self-healing patterns.
findings.gap: Incident response entirely manual and ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents. Start with markdown, then automate with Systems Manager.

findings.title: No observability ownership defined
findings.description: No per-service dashboards, no alarm owners, no CODEOWNERS, no team attribution on monitoring resources.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. Monitoring is reactive and fragmented.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS. Create per-service dashboards. Tag CloudWatch resources with Owner and Team.

findings.title: Inconsistent tagging with no cost allocation or enforcement
findings.description: Most resources tagged with Name. Some with Environment: Production. No cost allocation, owner, or team tags. No enforcement.
findings.gap: No cost allocation tags. Inconsistent coverage. No tag enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard. Use Terraform default_tags. Add AWS Config rules for compliance.

evaluations.title: Managed Compute
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — all compute runs on AWS App Runner (fully managed containerized service) with auto-scaling.

evaluations.title: Managed Databases
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — RDS MySQL 8.4.8 Multi-AZ with encrypted storage, IAM auth, enhanced monitoring, automated backups.

evaluations.title: High Availability and Fault Isolation
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — RDS Multi-AZ, App Runner inherently multi-AZ, VPC spans 2 AZs.

evaluations.title: Stored Procedures and Schema Complexity
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — no stored procedures, triggers, or proprietary SQL. All business logic in application layer.

evaluations.title: Audit Logging
evaluations.reason: Application-level IaC only (App Runner, RDS, VPC, WAF). CloudTrail is an account-level service — evaluation belongs in foundation infrastructure repo.

triggering_questions.note: All communication synchronous, no async patterns
All operations synchronous regardless of duration

immediate_actions: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition with inventory service

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=4 — compute already runs on App Runner (managed containerized service)

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1=4 meets threshold; already on managed container compute

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already runs on App Runner (managed containerized compute). Container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 — no stored procedures; MySQL is already open source

triggering_questions.note: No proprietary SQL; MySQL is open source

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. MySQL is already open source. No stored procedures or proprietary SQL.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=4 — database already on RDS (fully managed)

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q2=4 meets threshold; RDS MySQL fully managed with Multi-AZ

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Database is already on Amazon RDS (fully managed) with Multi-AZ, encrypted storage, and automated backups.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=1 < 3 but contextual guard blocks: no data processing workloads exist

triggering_questions.note: No messaging, but no data processing workloads to migrate to managed analytics

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. Simple CRUD e-commerce app with no data processing responsibilities. Contextual guard blocks pathway.

triggering_questions.note: No CI/CD pipeline — all deployments manual
Rolling deployments only, no canary or blue/green

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'agent' but none of the specific AI signal terms (agentic, LLM, Bedrock, generative AI, etc.) are present.
